description: Overnight dissemination of broker back-office data (accounts, balances, deals, transactions, entitlements) to subscribed institutional clients for reconciliation.

actors.name: Participating Institution
actors.description: Asset manager, pension fund, or other entity subscribing to a broker's disseminated data feed.

actors.name: Broker
actors.description: Back-office member whose account, balance, deal, and transaction data is disseminated to an authorised institution.

actors.name: Customer Support
actors.description: Exchange operations staff who configure the scheduled download (process DISINS) per institution's written request.

actors.name: Dissemination Engine
actors.description: Batch process that assembles the nightly file from the back-office system and writes to the institution's allocated dataset.

actors.name: Open Systems / Identity Provider
actors.description: Issues and maintains credentials (user ID, password, dataset name) used to retrieve the file.

general.rule_1: Institution must submit a written request to Customer Support to be scheduled on the dissemination process (DISINS).
general.rule_2: Each institution is allocated a dedicated dataset; data for multiple brokers may be aggregated into that dataset.
general.rule_3: Each disseminated broker must have explicit written authorisation on file before their data is released to an institution.
general.rule_4: All records in the file are fixed-width, 173 characters, blank-filled.
general.rule_5: Every file begins with a header record (machine date/time, batch date) and ends with a trailer record (record count).
general.rule_6: All balance amounts are multiplied by 100 and represented as whole numbers; sign character (+, -, or space) precedes the amount.
general.rule_7: Each detail record begins with SYSTEM ('BD') followed by a 2-digit CARD-CDE identifying the record layout.
general.rule_8: Passwords must be at least 8 characters, alphanumeric, unique per user, and must not contain common words or personal identifiers.
general.rule_9: Password resume/reset requires a signed letterhead request, callback verification, and is performed only by Open Systems.
general.rule_10: Selections in the DISINS request are hierarchical: BRANCH/PARTNER and PF-IND are applied first, then record-type selection flags.
general.rule_11: Disclosure of broker client personal information to an institution is a POPIA processing activity; lawful basis and data-subject notification must be established before the feed is enabled.
general.rule_12: The institution must retain the file only as long as needed for reconciliation and must apply equivalent protection to the PII it contains.
general.rule_13: Cross-border transfer of the file to an institution outside South Africa requires POPIA section 72 conditions to be met.
general.rule_14: Feeds must be auditable: every scheduled run, file produced, and file retrieval must be logged with timestamp, dataset, institution, and record count.
general.rule_15: Broker authorisation may be revoked in writing; next overnight run must exclude the revoked broker's data.

errors.message: Institution is not authorised to receive data for the requested broker.
Institution code is not registered for dissemination.
The supplied credentials are not valid for file retrieval.
Password does not meet complexity requirements.
Overnight file has not yet been generated for the requested batch date.
Trailer record count does not match number of detail records.
Lawful basis for disclosing broker client personal information has not been recorded.
A dissemination schedule already exists for this institution and broker combination.

schedule_requires_authorisation.description: A dissemination schedule cannot be created unless the broker has authorised the institution.

schedule_requires_popia_basis.description: Schedule creation is blocked if no lawful basis for PII disclosure to the institution is on file.

schedule_created.description: Customer Support schedules DISINS for an authorised institution with selection flags.

overnight_file_generated.description: Overnight batch produces the fixed-width file with header, detail records per selection, and trailer.

trailer_record_count_mismatch.description: If the trailer count does not equal the number of detail records written, the file is flagged and not released.

credentials_rejected.description: File retrieval with invalid credentials is refused and logged.

file_retrieved.description: Institution retrieves the generated file from the allocated dataset over the configured protocol.

reconciliation_completed.description: Institution reconciles deal and transaction records to in-house books and reports match status.

authorisation_revoked.description: When a broker revokes authorisation, the next batch excludes that broker's data and the institution is notified.

steps.step: Submit written request to Customer Support naming brokers to be disseminated.
Verify broker authorisation letters for each requested broker.
Issue user ID, password, and dataset name; confirm receipt with institution.
Schedule DISINS with the institution's selection flags.
Perform connectivity and decode test against the first generated file.
Read schedule, extract records per selection flags, write fixed-width 173-char records to institution's dataset.
Write header, detail records (ordered by card code), and trailer with record count.
Retrieve file via SFTP/FTPS/dataset after batch completion.
Parse records by CARD-CDE, decode signed/scaled balance fields, and load into back-office reconciliation system.
Reconcile deals (layouts 81-84, 99) and transactions (87, 88, 89, 97) against in-house systems; raise exceptions for unmatched items.
